title: Op-registry does not cover the library write surface — whole capabilities unreachable from the cli / out-of-pi
description: Library write capabilities have no op-registry entry, so they are unreachable from pi-context-cli and in-pi tools: edge removal/replace (removeRelation does not exist in the library at all; writeRelations whole-replace has no op), upsertItemInBlock (atomic find-or-replace), writeConfig (whole-config), writeSkeletonConfig, writeSchema/updateSchema (unchecked write + mutator), registerSubstrate/writeRegistry (foreign-substrate registration), writeBootstrapPointer/flipBootstrapPointer (raw pointer), and bulk appendRelations. An operator restricted to the cli cannot correct a mis-filed edge, run an atomic upsert, or write a config/registry/pointer.

evidence.reference: writeRelations (whole-relations replace) + appendRelations (bulk) have no op; removeRelation does not exist — relations are append-only via ops
upsertItemInBlock atomic find-or-replace — no op (used only by pi-behavior-monitors directly)
writeRegistry/registerSubstrate — no op; foreign-substrate registration unreachable from cli

impact: The cli is not a faithful interface to the library write surface; whole categories of substrate operation are out-of-pi-unreachable. FGAP-006 (no edge-removal op) is a specific instance, with the sub-layer that the removeRelation library primitive is itself missing.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
